title: Matelea aspera
intro: Matelea aspera, also known as the rough-stemmed Matelea or "yerba mate de monte", is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, commonly found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, Matelea aspera has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Matelea aspera include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Matelea aspera is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used as a component in traditional remedies, such as yerba mate blends and decoctions.
taxonomy: Matelea aspera is classified within the plant family Apocynaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other species. Within the Apocynaceae family, Matelea aspera belongs to the genus Matelea, which comprises various species of tropical vines and shrubs. The Apocynaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, and Matelea aspera is part of one of these subfamilies. Matelea aspera's classification provides important information about its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.
uses: Matelea aspera has been traditionally used by indigenous communities in South America for its medicinal properties. The plant is reported to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. Its extracts have been used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal problems. Additionally, Matelea aspera has been used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. More research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Matelea aspera for medicinal purposes.

side_effects: Matelea aspera, like other plants in the Apocynaceae family, may cause gastrointestinal upset, nausea, and vomiting if ingested. The plant's toxic compounds can also cause cardiac problems, including arrhythmias and hypotension. Ingestion of large amounts may lead to respiratory depression, and in severe cases, it can be fatal.
